name: Crime Responses

columns.name: Offense Day of Week
columns.description: Day of the week when the offense took place.

columns.name: ID
columns.description: Incident identification number.

columns.name: State
columns.description: State where the incident occurred.

columns.name: Address
columns.description: Location of the offense approximated to street block.

columns.name: Latitude
columns.description: Latitude of the street block where the offense took place.

columns.name: Longitude
columns.description: Longitude of the street block where the offense took place.

columns.name: Location
columns.description: Geo-coded point of the street block where the offense took place.

columns.name: City
columns.description: City where the incident occurred.

columns.name: Offense Hour of Day
columns.description: Hour of day when the offense took place.

columns.name: Report Day of Week
columns.description: Day of the week when the offense was reported.

columns.name: Report Hour of Day
columns.description: Hour of the day when the offense was reported.

columns.name: Offense Date
columns.description: Date and time of the offense.

columns.name: Report Date
columns.description: Date and time that the incident report was filed.

columns.name: Incident Type
columns.description: Type of incident that occured.

description: Disclaimer: Crime Responses is provided by the Gainesville Police Department (GPD) to document initial details surrounding an incident to which GPD officers respond. This dataset contains crime incidents from 2011 to present and includes a reduced set of fields focused on capturing the type of incident as well when and where an incident occurred. The Incident location addresses have been rounded off and are not the exact location due to the constitutional amendment known as "Marsy's Law". In 202

name: Traffic Crashes

columns.description: Crash case number

columns.name: Location
columns.description: The accident location, as determined by reporting officer, as derived from the reported address of crash, in a column type that allows for mapping and other geographic analysis in the data portal software

columns.name: At/From Intersection
columns.description: At or From intersection where crash occured

columns.description: Street address direction (N-North,E-East,,S-South,W-West) of crash location

columns.description: Distance from a reference point(exact address or intersection) where crash occured

description: This dataset shows information about each traffic crash since 2011 on city streets within the City of Gainesville limits and under the jurisdiction of Gainesville Police Department (GPD). Data shown are more than 60 days from when accidents occurred due to Florida Statutes 316.066. Disclaimer: In 2021, Florida reporting of crime data began a transition from Summary Reporting System (SRS) to National Incident-Based Reporting System (NIBRS), causing an effect on crime statistics reported by Law En

name: Code Complaints, Violations & Permits

columns.name: Infraction Date
columns.description: Date when violation identified or investigation occurred.

columns.name: 2010 Census Tracts
columns.description: This column was automatically created in order to record in what polygon from the dataset '2010 Census Tracts' (e6r8-dw75) the point in column 'geocode' is located.  This enables the creation of region maps (choropleths) in the visualization canvas and data lens.

columns.name: City Commission Districts
columns.description: This column was automatically created in order to record in what polygon from the dataset 'City Commission Districts' (4x5c-ywwx) the point in column 'geocode' is located.  This enables the creation of region maps (choropleths) in the visualization canvas and data lens.

columns.name: Case Status
columns.description: Current status of response to complaint received.

columns.name: Case Type
columns.description: Description of case per the complaint received or violation identified.

columns.name: Address
columns.description: Address of the complaint or violation.

description: The Code Enforcement Division enforces: - Housing and Commercial Building Codes (Chapter 13) - Rental Housing Permits (Chapter 14.5) - Nuisance Codes (Chapter 16) - Abandoned Vehicles on private property (Chapter 26) - Land Development Code (Chapter 30) - Graffiti (Chapter 13) This report lists, code complaints, violations and permits. Data is updated monthly and dates back to February 2011. Please note that addresses that are listed under the Location field as 0 W 1ST ST are null place-holders

name: Fire/Rescue Responses

columns.name: Responding Unit
columns.description: Name and type of GFR unit responding to call, including E (engines), Q (quints), SQ (squads), and T (towers).

columns.name: Location Name
columns.description: Name of location if the response was to a general area, building, or business.

columns.name: Response Type
columns.description: Type of response to call-for-service, including EMS (emergency medical services), ALM (alarm), Fire, and SVC (other general services).

columns.name: Response Date
columns.description: Date and time GFR responds to a call for service.

columns.name: Master Incident Number
columns.description: Unique number describing the response.

description: Fire / EMS response data for FY2012 up to the most current month available.

name: 311 Service Requests (myGNV)

columns.name: Acknowledged
columns.description: The date and time of when the service request was acknowledged by staff through the online 311 system

columns.name: Last updated
columns.description: The date and time of the last update made to the service request

columns.name: Reopened
columns.description: The date and time of when the service request was reopened by the requester

columns.name: Closed
columns.description: The date and time of when the service request was "closed" by staff. This reflects whether the request was moved into a work order to solve the issue or if the ticket was resolved with the requester

columns.name: Minutes to acknowledge
columns.description: The amount of time (in minutes) to acknowledge the open request with a message through the online 311 system

columns.name: Assigned To:
columns.description: The department or office the request has been assigned to

columns.name: Address
columns.description: The address of the issue (manually input by the requester)

columns.name: Minutes to close
columns.description: The amount of time (in minutes) to resolve the  request with a message through the online 311 system

columns.name: Service Request Date
columns.description: The date and time the service request was made

columns.name: Description
columns.description: Detailed description of the service request

columns.name: ID
columns.description: The unique identification number for each open service request

columns.name: Reporter Display
columns.description: The chosen display name for those reporting. May be a user name (logged in an using their user name to report), anonymous (logged in but choosing to stay anonymous) or guest (not signed in)

columns.name: Status
columns.description:
Status of the request. 
OPEN:  The request has been sent
ACKNOWLEDGED: Staff has acknowledged that the request has been opened.
CLOSED: The service request has either been made into a work order to fix the problem or the request has been resolved with the requester. 
REOPENED: The request has been reopened by the requester for further investigation

columns.name: Reported using mobile?
columns.description: If the issue was reported through a mobile device

description: Non-emergency requests like pothole repairs or damaged street signs can be reported through the Web: www.myGNV.org or app. iPhone Users: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/mygnv/id1011272233 Android Users: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.citysourced.gainesvillefl or online (http://www.cityofgainesville.org/openGNV/311GNV.aspx). ***Disclaimer: The data reflected on this page is through 06/18/2021. We have updated our MyGNV app. We are working to get the data uploaded from the new pla

description: "This dataset includes the raw results from the City of Gainesville 2021 Neighborhood Survey. For reference to columns within this dataset, please view the survey, given here: https://tinyurl.com/yxye5ese Responses of "9" for questions on a 1-5 scale indicate a non-response or a response of "Don't know".

name: City of Gainesville Parking Lots
description: The downtown Gainesville area provides free time limited parking as well as metered and long term parking. This dataset contains spatial locations and selected attributes for off-street parking facilities in the downtown and university areas maintained by the City of Gainesville.

description: This dataset includes the raw results from the City of Gainesville 2020 Neighborhood Survey. Please view the survey, given here: https://tinyurl.com/CoGNeighborSurvey for reference to columns within this dataset. Responses of "9" for questions on a 1-5 scale indicate a non-response or a response of "Don't know".

name: Annexation Activity

columns.name: Development
columns.description: Whether the property was developed or undeveloped at the time of annexation.

columns.name: Yes Votes
columns.description: If the annexation type was referendum, this is the amount of votes in favor of the annexation.

columns.name: Effective Date
columns.description: If the annexation was successful, this is the date that it officially became part of Gainesville.

columns.name: Success
columns.description: If the annexation was successful.

columns.name: Year
columns.description: Year that the annexation became effective.

columns.name: No Votes
columns.description: If the annexation type was referendum, this is the amount of votes not in favor of the annexation.

description: Annexation is when unincorporated property becomes incorporated into the city. This data set contains annexation activity beginning in 1985 to present. Explore the data lens associated with this data set to experience this data through different visualizations.

name: Asset Inventory

columns.name: Last Update Date (data)
columns.description: Last update of tabular data only, will not reflect metadata or shapefiles

columns.name: Derived View
columns.description: if the row represents a view/map/data_lens page etc. that is derived from another dataset = True

columns.name: Public
columns.description: Public or Private

columns.name: Publication Stage
columns.description:
Has four possible values:

* Unpublished: Does not show up in catalog. Waiting to be published.
* Published: The authoritative and broadly available current copy of a dataset
* Snapshotted: An older copy of the dataset which has been saved for reference
* Copying: A dataset which is in the process of being copied internally. This is an ephemeral state.

columns.name: Contact Email
columns.description: contact email for the dataset listed in private metadata

columns.name: Domain
columns.description: if data is federated, the source domain will populate here

columns.name: Parent UID
columns.description: if the row represents a view/map/data_lens page etc. the underlying parent dataset UID populates

columns.name: Published Version UID
columns.description: The uid of the currently published version of this dataset

columns.name: Published Version Name
columns.description: The name of the currently published version of this dataset

columns.name: Owner
columns.description: Dataset Owner Name

description: This dataset is an inventory of the assets on the site. Here you can track the performance of data publishing, metadata maintenance, as well as present an overview of what data exists on the site. Note: This is only updated once per day.

name: TrafficData20140522
description: The Transportation Planning Division is responsible for the planning, design, operation and maintenance necessary to provide an effective and safe transportation network for vehicular, bicycle and pedestrian traffic. This dataset contains selected attributes and spatial locations of traffic count sites in the city of Gainesville owned and operated by the city, county and state.

name: PlanningTest.DBO.MunicipalBoundary_data
description: The municipal boundary presents the political boundary for the City of Gainesville, FL. The municipal boundary describes what parcels are in Gainesville. This impacts property taxes, utility service charges, and emergency response for properties. The municipal boundary may be changed through the passage of an ordinance by the City Commission.

name: Stormwater_Features
description: For NPDES Stormwater sewer system enhanced mapping project. Contains a GIS polygon feature class of stormwater basins in Gainesville, FL as a result of the NPDES stormwater system mapping project. This feature does not participate in the GIS network, and is for cartographic purposes only. This file is current only up to 02/04/08 and may be incomplete, and only covers those areas of Gainesville, FL that have been mapped up to 02/04/08. The file is also subject to constant updating as project prog

name: City_Parks
description: This layer details the location, size, and type of parks/natural areas in the City of Gainesville.

name: Homeless Survey Zones_data
description: Homeless Survey Zones as defined by: Theresa Theresa Lowe, Executive Director tlowe@gracemarketplace.org North Central Florida Coalition for the Homeless and Hungry Operating GRACE Marketplace 3055 NE 28th Drive Gainesville, FL 32609 (352) 792-0800, ext. 105 www.gracemarketplace.org

name: PlanningTest.DBO.ExistingZoning_data
description: The existing zoning classifications are defined at the parcel-level. The zoning classifications for Gainesville are outlined in Article IV of the Land Development Code, which is Chapter 30 of the City’s Code of Ordinances. More broadly, the zoning districts are established to implement the Comprehensive Plan of the City, to promote orderly urban growth, and to classify and regulate the use of land, water, buildings, and structures within the city. For more information, please review Article IV o

name: Government Owned Parcels_data
description: Parcels owned by the City of Gainesville, Alachua County or the State of Florida.

name: PlanningTest.DBO.ExistingLandUse_data
description: The existing land use categories are defined at the parcel-level. Land use categories are established by the Future Land Use Element of the city’s Comprehensive Plan. Extended descriptions of the land use categories can be found in the Goals, Objectives, and Policies document under Policy 4.1.1. Land use categories were implemented to allow for appropriate uses at suitable locations to meet the needs of current and projected populations. They also give the City flexibility to consider unique, in

name: layer_0
description: Regional Transit System bus stops and associated amenities. This data set was developed in order to maintain a geospatial inventory of all City of Gainesville Regional Transit System bus stops and associated amenities. This dataset containes information about the location, attributes and details of each individual bus stop, currently, previously or proposed in the City of Gainesville Regional Transit System.

name: cghistdist
description: Historical Preservation District is established for the purpose of identifying, classifying and protecting sites, buildings, structures, objects and districts that are historically and/or architecturally significant. Description This shapefile represents boundaries of the 5 Historic Districts located in the City of Gainesville.

name: On_Street_Parking_Meters
description: Parking Operations maintains and collects over 400 parking meters in the downtown area and university context area. This dataset contains the spatial locations and selected attributes of a majority of these parking meters.

name: cghiststruct
description: Footprint of buildings located in the Historic Preservation Districts.

name: Lights
provider: City of Gainesville - Public Works Department
description: LED street lights that are installed and maintained by Public Works. This is not a comprehensive list of all Public Works street lights and is currently missing the lights in Innovation Square and on Depot Avenue.

name: Creeks
description: Contains polyline shapefile of the streams and creeks within the Gainesville area. Infomation was collected by ground surveys, old maps, GPS, and using lidar contours to digitize creek locations.

name: Census_Tract_City_of_Gainesville_2010_Pop
description: GIS data: Boundaries of 2010 Census Tracts

name: Voting_Precincts_Projected
description: Boundaries of the voting precincts with designated voting location for Alachua county.

name: HUBZone_10282014
description: HUBZone areas are defined by the Federal Government’s Small Business Administration (SBA). The Historically Underutilized Business Zones (HUBZone) program helps small businesses in urban and rural communities gain preferential access to federal procurement opportunities.

name: Facility Site Points_data
description: Points that represent significant facility locations in a community. Examples include schools, government facilities, stadiums, casinos, and fire stations. In some cases, there may be multiple FacilitySitePoints for a single FacilitySite polygon. Additional information about each Facility can be organized in to seperate tables and related to these locations using the FacilityID key. The feature classification and type schema for the facility sites grew out of work with the Department of Homeland

name: Parking_Enforcement_Areas
description: This dataset contains parking enforement areas for the city of gainesville. Parking within these areas are restricted and usually require neighborhood parking decals during partiular hours of the day.

name: Block_City_of_Gainesville_2010
tags: 2010 census blocks & tracts gis: 2010 census blocks
description: GIS data: Boundaries of 2010 Census Blocks

name: PlanningTest.DBO.GainesvilleWetlands_data
description: Wetlands in Gainesville

name: PlanningTest.DBO.AnnexationHistory_data
description: This layer presents the annexation history for Gainesville, delineating areas by year they were annexed into the city. The type of annexation, like voluntary or referendum, is also included for each area. New areas can be annexed by the city through the different annexation methods, but final approval must be made by the city commission through the passage of an ordinance.

name: ZCTA_City_of_Gainesville_2010
description: Boundaries for Gainesville City zip codes.

name: GPDZones_101518_Dissolved
description: Gainesville Police Department has 2 districts, each district has 2 sectors and each sector has 3 zones. These are designated patrol areas for GPD officers.
